name: Chicken with Porcini Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 pounds chicken pieces, skinned if desired
Fresh herb (basal or thyme) snipped -- optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse and drain dried mushrooms.
2. Soak the mushrooms in warm water for 20 to 30 minutes.
3. Drain the mushrooms, reserving the liquid.
4. Coarsely chop the mushrooms; set aside.
5. In a 12-inch skillet, heat butter and olive oil over medium-high heat.
6. Add chicken pieces and cook about 10 minutes or until browned on both sides (add more oil if necessary).
7. Add wine, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper. Simmer, uncovered, for about 5 minutes or until most of the wine has evaporated.
8. Add mushrooms and chopped tomatoes. Slowly add reserved mushroom liquid.
9. Cover skillet; reduce heat to low. Simmer for 15 to 20 minutes or until chicken is tender and no longer pink.
10. Transfer chicken to a warm platter.
11. For sauce, skim fat from the mixture in the skillet. Bring to a boil; boil for about 5 minutes or until desired consistency. Pour over chicken.
12. Serve with tomatoes sprinkled with freshly grated Parmesan and herb (s).
